In the CPS Procedural Manual, it states, "The IEP team then submits a school assignment request to ODLSS to provide the appropriate school closest to the student’s residence that can implement the IEP." 

Provide records sufficient to show the following: 

1. Anonymized data of all school assignment requests made on behalf of CPS students with IEPs requiring placement for the 2025-2026 (to date) school year. These records should include a unique non-identifiable code for the student, the date their IEP was finalized, the date the school assignment request was made, the date the school assignment letter was issued, and the date the student enrolled. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2199523/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N018913-012526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
02/24/2026 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N018913-012526 
Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: 
To Whom It May Concern: 
Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 
In the CPS Procedural Manual, it states, "The IEP team then submits a school assignment request to ODLSS to provide the appropriate school closest to the student’s residence that can implement the IEP." 
Provide records sufficient to show the following: 
1. Anonymized data of all school assignment requests made on behalf of CPS students with IEPs requiring placement for the 2025-2026 (to date) school year. These records should include a unique non-identifiable code for the student, the date their IEP was finalized, the date the school assignment request was made, the date the school assignment letter was issued, and the date the student enrolled. 
This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
Sincerely, 
Miriam Bhimani 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2199523/ 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please be advised that the District does not comprehensively track the date a student enrolled as a result of a school assignment request. Thus, this portion of your request is asking the District to create a new record to answer your question. The Freedom of Information Act requires a public body to provide existing public records, subject to certain exceptions. The request must reasonably describe the requested records. A reasonable description requires the record to be reasonably identified as a record not as a general request for data, information and statistics to be gleaned generally from documents which have not been created and which the [public body] does not generally create. (See Krohn v. Department of Justice, 628 F.2d 195 (1980)). Second,  FOIA obligations apply to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request. Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32,    540 N.E.2d 11, 13 (1989) (quoting Krohn v. Department of Justice, 628 F.2d 195, 198 (D.C. Cir. 1980)). This request requires CPS to not merely pull data out of a database and provide it to you, but instead asks CPS to compile data from multiple sources into a single record, manipulate it to answer your questions, and this combination of data does not already exist in any CPS database. (See generally Hites v. Waubonsee Comm. College, Il App 2d, 150836 (2016)(... "requests went beyond a search for records—that is, the data in the databases—and instead improperly sought information about those records." quoting National Security Counselors v. Central Intelligence Agency, 898 F. Supp. 2d 233, 271 (D.D.C. 2012) and Chicago Tribune v. DFPR, IL App. (4th) 130437 at 8 (2014) ("[t]he Department was not obligated ... to answer plaintiff's "general inquiry question" concerning the number of initial claims since this would have required creating a new record.") 
You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Marisol Muneton Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

Attachments: 
R010421_-_final.pdf (https://u8387795.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.PYICgxP3kfgZjR-2B4e9nPlOQ-2Bei55-2BIGqwpXIOk6BH6dm2kHnPUEryqozXjw8DgvJ9NWaxWuNgoDNHJc9Y6c9BJmhcXO5zNQ2z4f6-2FWi-2BpjbLlpnd9IbT2FuY5ynovugzt4EyaSV1JdGB2wYADDM6J1rzMtmbgK7qJXTKbKmOtRmDjYdYxS-2B6hwYMrygcYtosWQrEtawedEAJ8m7s-2B44snA-3D-3DQwGB_3KHBpV8V-2BpPLZ1JIHpX-2Bz8iTe0LB1u7dyNgRqxMW9oRF-2Fr9ew-2FbgRfh-2BWIn8SUyefRSNON5re4hFDg5eZF4uivIeCMBZTLsCGtoSAy0vXjbcI0ngOGsDztIe-2FSpTsQnX66eIPSUkWz24B-2B9NIe-2FlyHGe1vbsuh-2B-2Bil1VyDrKGds2lbx-2BJ4oi0J3IdjC2uQs4r2OflIdgg0qj7WnPqOmLJNrLIbMfxW3-2Fkx9kGwLz0ntDyUKgp2bQ4e49hST8Z7N8-2BU3B5wpVgTrABTWZKJUQ3ECKUjSCew0OLm-2B-2BO0-2BPvl4dPpALaeVUqIRHGcBGw8T97SNdN2-2BGqw-2BD34LTlpmxTMatEvj4D3CPmPU91W58zqtckTSUuJdNgFBDuuRBahQCRL6Pf50KXor3ZLFezH7cuw-3D-3D) 

--- Please respond above this line --- 

Reference: Rutgers Open Public Records Request, # R010421-121725 
Dear Mrs. Yasinski, 
Please accept this letter as the University Custodian of Records response to your request for records pursuant to the Open Public Records Act (“OPRA”). On December 17, 2025, this office received your request for the following: 
“To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Communications, including emails, reports, texts, phone records, between New Jersey Poison Information and Education System (NJPIES) and the FDA or CDC referencing yellow oleander, tejocote, tejocote root, hawthorn root, raiz de tejocote, or brazil nut supplements from Sept 7, 2022 to Dec 1, 2025. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Emma Yasinski 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2158901/” 
The University has reviewed your updated request. The responsive document(s) is now available to you through the Rutgers University Open Records Center. Please log in to the Portions of the documents you seek are advisory, consultative and deliberative. OPRA specifically states that government records “shall not include inter-agency or intra-agency advisory, consultative, or deliberative material.” Records Center and "My Records Center" to retrieve the appropriate responsive document(s). The document(s) is also attached to this email for your convenience.  N.J.S.A. 47:1A-1.1. Courts have construed this exemption to encompass the deliberative process privilege. See Ciesla v. N.J. Dept. of Health & Senior Servs., 429 N.J. Super. 127, 137 (App. Div. 2012). That privilege “‘permits the government to withhold documents that reflect advisory opinions, recommendations, and deliberations comprising part of a process by which governmental decisions and policies are formulated.’” Education Law Center. v. New Jersey Dept. of Education, 198 N.J. 274, 285-86 (2009) (quoting In re Liquidation of Integrity Ins. Co., 165 N.J. 75, 83 (2000)). Moreover, the deliberative process privilege is absolute, as OPRA “contains no limitation or qualification on this exemption.” Ciesla, 429 N.J. Super. at 143. 

Moreover, the Courts have consistently held that the advisory, consultative and deliberative exemption in OPRA is equivalent to the deliberative process privilege that exempts pre-decisional documents from public disclosure. OPRA thus “shields from disclosure documents ‘deliberative in nature, containing opinions, recommendations, or advice about agency policies,’ and ‘generated before the adoption of an agency’s policy or decision.’” Bent v. Stafford Police Department, 381 N.J. Super. 30, 37 (App. Div. 2005)(quoting Gannet New Jersey Partners LP v. County of Middlesex, 379 N.J. Super. 205, 219 (App. Div. 2005)). 

If your request has been denied, you may take your appeal to the New Jersey Government Records Council (NJGRC), which has mediation and dispute resolution procedures, or you may file a lawsuit with the New Jersey Superior Court, as provided by N.J.S.A. 47:1A-1, et seq. Your rights of appeal is provided with this letter at Public Facing Pages of the Rutgers University Open Public Records Request Center . You may also review the brochure at the Rutgers Custodian of Records page or visit the NJGRC page. 
If you have any questions, or wish to discuss this further, you may contact my office. 

Sincerely, 
Mary Ann Keys 
Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey

title: Marvin Day interaction reports

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Marvin Day interaction reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Regarding an interaction with Marvin Day on 07/02/2015:
- All incident, dispatch log entries and arrest reports. Incident reports and case records refer to reports produced with notes and narratives, a description of what happened by the responding officer, trace reports, case notes and litigation files
- All unedited body-worn camera / bodycam recordings. I am specifically requesting the unedited body-worn camera recordings captured by all officers present for the full duration of the incident, including audio recordings associated with the footage. If any portion of the requested footage must be redacted, I ask that you clearly indicate the portions that have been redacted and provide an explanation of the legal basis for the redactions
- All 911 or calls for service logs, audio and transcripts
- All computer aided dispatch (CAD) reports and a copy of your department’s dispatch 10 signals/codes, agency dispatch signals/codes and CAD signals/codes

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, I additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

I am a member of the news media. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Production of Responsive Records on a Rolling Basis: In the event that a portion of the present request is completed before the full request is completed, I ask that the records be produced on a rolling basis.

Request for Explanation of Withholdings and Redactions: If this request is denied in whole or in part, please provide a reasonable description of any withheld materials and a justification for all such withholdings that includes reference to the specific  exemptions authorizing withholding and specific reasons why such exemptions apply. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

RE: Public Records Request, dated August 10, 2025, Reference # P218832-081025 
Dear Dillon Bergin: 
The San Francisco Police Department (SFPD) received your Public Records Act request, dated August 10, 2025. 
You requested, "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Regarding an interaction with Marvin Day on 07/02/2015: 
- All incident, dispatch log entries and arrest reports. Incident reports and case records refer to reports produced with notes and narratives, a description of what happened by the responding officer, trace reports, case notes and litigation files 
- All unedited body-worn camera / bodycam recordings. I am specifically requesting the unedited body-worn camera recordings captured by all officers present for the full duration of the incident, including audio recordings associated with the footage. If any portion of the requested footage must be redacted, I ask that you clearly indicate the portions that have been redacted and provide an explanation of the legal basis for the redactions 
- All 911 or calls for service logs, audio and transcripts 
- All computer aided dispatch (CAD) reports and a copy of your department’s dispatch 10 signals/codes, agency dispatch signals/codes and CAD signals/codes 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, I additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations. 

I am a member of the news media. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

Production of Responsive Records on a Rolling Basis: In the event that a portion of the present request is completed before the full request is completed, I ask that the records be produced on a rolling basis. 

Request for Explanation of Withholdings and Redactions: If this request is denied in whole or in part, please provide a reasonable description of any withheld materials and a justification for all such withholdings that includes reference to the specific exemptions authorizing withholding and specific reasons why such exemptions apply. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Dillon Bergin 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fsan-francisco-police-department-367%252Fmarvin-day-interaction-reports-190925%252F%253Femail%253Dsanfranciscopd%252540govqa.us&url_auth_token=AAAIhlqz_6HzFpF9QbtVNXCgHbA%3A1ulIvR%3Adk9kil_L-v9wyio-oZwz0jAJU_9Tm1wSUcZj-3U-7Eg" 
The Legal and BWC (Body Worn Camera) Units are experiencing a heavy backlog of PRA requests. Each document (“documents” include but are not limited to photos, BWC, physical copies of records, and electronically stored records) must be reviewed for exemptions to the CPRA, and those exemptions must be redacted before our release to you. When the SFPD determines that responsive and non-exempt documents are available, we will produce them to you as soon as practicable and, if necessary, on an incremental or rolling basis. 
Requests that are time sensitive, such as those with pending court dates, claims, any additional legal matters, etc.- are given priority. Please advise if you have an upcoming court hearing or if you only need documents from specific officers to expedite your request. 
Thank you for your understanding in this regard. For further information or questions, please contact Robert Rueca at [ASSIGNED_STAFF_EMAIL]. 
Sincerely, 
Media Relations

I am specifically requesting the unedited body-worn camera recordings captured by all officers present for the full duration of the incident, including audio recordings associated with the footage. If any portion of the requested footage must be redacted, I ask that you clearly indicate the portions that have been redacted and provide an explanation of the legal basis for the redactions 
- All 911 or calls for service logs, audio and transcripts 
- All computer aided dispatch (CAD) reports and a copy of your department’s dispatch 10 signals/codes, agency dispatch signals/codes and CAD signals/codes 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, I additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations. 

I am a member of the news media. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

Production of Responsive Records on a Rolling Basis: In the event that a portion of the present request is completed before the full request is completed, I ask that the records be produced on a rolling basis. 

Request for Explanation of Withholdings and Redactions: If this request is denied in whole or in part, please provide a reasonable description of any withheld materials and a justification for all such withholdings that includes reference to the specific exemptions authorizing withholding and specific reasons why such exemptions apply. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Dillon Bergin

title: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robin Monks

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Albany County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Albany County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Financial Report Request (Old Dominion University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Financial Report Request (Old Dominion University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Request for Math department records (California Polytechnic University Pomona)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Request for Math department records (California Polytechnic University Pomona)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All documents and records from the past two (2) years related to any agreements between the University and any of the following vendors in connection with usage for Math courses at the University:
* Cengage (aka Cengage Learning, Inc.), including any contracts related to WebAssign.
* Pearson (aka Pearson Education, Inc.), including any contracts related to MyLab.
* Wiley (aka John Wiley & Sons, Inc.) including any contracts related to WileyPlus or Knewton/Knewton Alta.
* McGraw Hill (aka McGraw Hill LLC, formerly McGraw-Hill Education, Inc.), including any contracts related to ALEKS.
* Lumen (aka Lumen Learning, Inc.), including any contracts related to Lumen OHM

2. All records identifying any individual who served as a course coordinator for any Math course at the University during the past two (2) years. This request is limited to official role-based information, including names, titles, and institutional (work) contact information of individuals assigned as course coordinators or holding equivalent coordinator responsibilities within the Math department. This request does not seek personal information beyond what appears in institutional records relating to job duties or course assignments. 

If any responsive records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide the specific legal basis for each withholding and a description of the withheld material as required under applicable public records law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sidhanth Grover

I wanted to follow up on the following California Public Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Nov. 24, 2025. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Public Records Act Request: Grover, Sidhanth_PRA_20251124
communications.communication:
[cid:image001.png@01DC6512.25BFB650]
|    3801 W. Temple Ave, Pomona, CA 91768


December 4, 2025

RE: Public Records Act Request

Dear Sidhanth Grover,

We received your November 24, 2025, Public Records Act request for the following records:

1. All documents and records from the past two (2) years related to any agreements between the University and any of the following vendors in connection with usage for Math courses at the University:
* Cengage (aka Cengage Learning, Inc.), including any contracts related to WebAssign.
* Pearson (aka Pearson Education, Inc.), including any contracts related to MyLab.
* Wiley (aka John Wiley & Sons, Inc.) including any contracts related to WileyPlus or Knewton/Knewton Alta.
* McGraw Hill (aka McGraw Hill LLC, formerly McGraw-Hill Education, Inc.), including any contracts related to ALEKS.
* Lumen (aka Lumen Learning, Inc.), including any contracts related to Lumen OHM

2. All records identifying any individual who served as a course coordinator for any Math course at the University during the past two (2) years. This request is limited to official role-based information, including names, titles, and institutional (work) contact information of individuals assigned as course coordinators or holding equivalent coordinator responsibilities within the Math department. This request does not seek personal information beyond what appears in institutional records relating to job duties or course assignments.


Cal Poly Pomona has conducted a reasonable search for these records under California Government Code §7922.535 and has determined that your request, either in whole or in part, seeks copies of disclosable public records. We will make available to you any that are public Cal. Govt. Code §§7927.700 and 7922.000.  Records subject to the following exemptions will be withheld or redacted as appropriate:

  *   Vendor Identification Number pursuant to Cal. Govt. Code §7928.715
  *   General Privacy Rights. Cal. Govt. Code §7927.705; Cal. Const. Art. 1, §1.
  *   Balancing Test. Records where the public interest against disclosure outweighs the public interest in disclosure. Cal. Govt. Code §7922.000.

We will let you know when we have gathered all the records. It will be necessary to redact from them any personal and otherwise privileged information before they can be made available to you. Cal Poly Pomona estimates the records will be available January 29, 2025.

If you wish to inspect the records at Cal Poly Pomona you may contact publicrecords@cpp.edu<publicrecords@cpp.edu%20> to schedule a mutually convenient date and time. If you wish to receive copies instead, Cal Poly Pomona may charge the direct cost of duplication (20 cents per page). Once the records are located, we will advise you as to the exact number of pages upon which any charge will be based. We will require full payment before we begin the redaction and duplication process.

Thank you,
CPP Public Records Request

Email: publicrecords@cpp.edu<mailto:publicrecords@cpp.edu>

This communication is intended only for the use of the individual or entity to which it is addressed.
